findings.summary: KiCad 6+ hall effect sensor board. Small design with 7 components and 5 nets. 1 signal path detected (design observation about missing decoupling cap). Component extraction and net connectivity are correct. This is a simple but well-analyzed design.

correct.description: All 7 components correctly extracted: HX6286 hall sensor, LED, 1k resistor, solder jumper, 3-pin connector, 2 mounting holes
Component types correctly classified: ic, led, resistor, jumper, connector, mounting_hole
Net connectivity correct: VDD, GND, SIGNAL nets with proper pin associations
LED indicator circuit correctly traced: VDD -> JP1 (solder jumper) -> R1 (1k) -> D1 (LED) -> SIGNAL
Decoupling observation correctly notes U1 VDD rail has no bypass capacitor
LCSC part numbers extracted for D1 (C2286), R1 (C21190), U1 (C495736)
Footprint filter warnings correctly flag custom library footprints not matching standard filter patterns
Assembly complexity correctly rated as low (score 36/100)

incorrect.description: SIGNAL net listed as a power rail for U1 in power_domains - the VOUT pin is open-collector output, not a power rail. SIGNAL should be classified as signal/output only

PWR_FLAG warnings claim GND and VDD have no PWR_FLAG, but the schematic has 2 PWR_FLAG symbols visible in power_symbols section. The PWR_FLAG detection is not recognizing them as satisfying the ERC requirement

missed.description: Hall sensor output circuit (open-collector with indicator LED) not detected as a signal pattern. The HX6286 VOUT drives LED indicator through solder jumper and series resistor - this is a common hall sensor indicator topology
The solder jumper JP1 as an LED enable/disable feature is not noted as a design pattern

suggestions: Open-collector sensor output with indicator LED is a common pattern worth detecting
Fix power_domains to not classify open-collector outputs as power rails
Fix PWR_FLAG detection to properly recognize existing PWR_FLAG symbols
